title: Secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN, Variables, Permissions, and Injection Defense · Independent Delivery Trial
summary: An independent delivery team receives an unfamiliar repository, event, runner, and trust constraint and must transfer secrets, github_token, variables, permissions, and injection defense into a new workflow authority and data-flow policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of workflow authority and data-flow policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Classify public configuration, non-secret variables, sensitive values, credentials, OIDC identity, and derived secrets by authority and lifetime.
Set default no or read authority and grant job-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN permissions only for exact API mutations.
Pass untrusted context through environment or structured input, quote at the receiving language, validate shape, and avoid generated shell or code.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Ci Secret Variable Config Classification
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab job grants write-all, expands an issue title in a shell, prints a transformed secret, and sends a token to a mutable action. Reduce permissions, separate data from code, rotate exposure, and retain redacted evidence. Competency probe: Classify public configuration, non-secret variables, sensitive values, credentials, OIDC identity, and derived secrets by authority and lifetime.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-secret-scope-rotation-incident-nv9eky". Tailor its authority-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident, keep Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-secret-scope-rotation-incident-nv9eky
  - competency: ci-secret-scope-rotation-incident
    artifact: authority-policy.yaml
    decision: secret scope rotation incident
    environment: multi-platform Buildx release to a non-production registry
    fault-injection: remove one supported Node and operating-system matrix cell
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: nv9eky
    change: |
      default-permissions: none
      checkout-job: contents-read
      attest-job: contents-read-id-token-write-attestations-write
      public-config: repository-variable
      secret: environment-scoped
      untrusted-input: validated-env-boundary
      shell: no-generated-code
      derived-secret: explicitly-masked
      rotation: rehearsed
    verify: trace every value from event and secret store through expression environment shell process log artifact cache and API mutation
    failure: Reject write-all defaults, expression-to-shell interpolation, masking as access control, transformed-secret auto-masking, or log deletion as response.
    repair: Classify data, grant per-job authority, validate untrusted input, prevent sinks, mask derived values, revoke, rotate, and test absence.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab job grants write-all, expands an issue title in a shell, prints a transformed secret, and sends a token to a mutable action. Reduce permissions, separate data from code, rotate exposure, and retain redacted evidence. Competency probe: Limit secrets by repository, environment, job, event, and actor; rotate on exposure; revoke sessions; preserve redacted evidence; and test absence.

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident, keep Ci Secret Scope Rotation Incident connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
